data.hero_alt: Yacht sailing at golden hour off the Spanish Mediterranean coast
data.meta_description: Sunset cruise Marbella from €250 for 2 hours: yachts and catamarans along the Golden Mile, departure 1h before sunset, skipper, drinks and snacks included.
data.summary: Sunset cruise spoke: timing tied to sunset, route along the Golden Mile, which boat type fits best, prices and what's included.
key_facts: Sunset cruise Marbella from €250 for 2 hours including skipper, fuel and drinks
Departure 60-75 minutes before sunset; check local time (sunset varies 18:30 December to 21:55 June)
Standard route: Puerto Banús westwards past the Golden Mile and back
Catamarans and 12 m motor yachts are the two most popular sunset boats
Couples and groups of 4 are the most common booking; capacity ranges from 2 to 30

faq.q: What time does the sunset cruise leave?
faq.a: 60–75 minutes before sunset, so you're at anchor with a drink in hand as the sun touches the horizon. Sunset times: December around 18:30, March 19:30, June 21:55, September 20:45. We confirm the exact departure time the day before.

faq.q: How long is a typical sunset cruise?
faq.a: Two hours is the default and the most popular length — enough to leave the marina, cruise the Golden Mile, anchor briefly at the best viewpoint, and return as the light fades. Three-hour cruises are available (€350 instead of €250) for groups who want a longer dinner on board.

faq.q: Which boat is best for sunset?
faq.a: For couples and small groups (2–6), a 10–12 m motor yacht with comfortable cushions on the bow is the classic choice. For groups of 8 or more, a sail or power catamaran gives you more deck space and a flatter platform for photos. Avoid sportfishers and licence-free runabouts — they're working boats, not romantic ones.

faq.q: Can we have champagne and food on board?
faq.a: Yes. BYO champagne, prosecco or wine is welcomed on most boats (cava is the local choice and travels best). Catered platters (charcuterie, sushi, hot tapas) can be added for €25–€60 per head, ordered 24 hours in advance. The boat's fridge will be chilled and ready.

Cloudy Marbella sunsets are often more dramatic than clear ones — the colour spreads across the sky for 30+ minutes. The trip runs unless wind exceeds Force 4–5 (rare in summer evenings). Full refund or rebook for genuine weather cancellations.

data.body_html:
The Marbella sunset cruise is two hours, leaves an hour before the sun touches the horizon, and ends just as the lights come on along the Golden Mile. From €250 with skipper, fuel, drinks and snacks included — and BYO cava is welcomed on every boat we work with.

When the sunset cruise actually leaves

Departure is 60–75 minutes before official sunset, so you're at anchor with a drink in hand when the colour starts. Local sunset times by month:

MonthSunset (Marbella)Cruise departs
December18:3017:15
March19:3018:15
May21:2520:10
June21:5520:40
July21:4520:30
August21:1019:55
September20:4519:30
October19:4518:30

Plan to arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure — there's no "please wait for us" on a sunset booking when the actual sun is on a fixed schedule.

The standard route

From Puerto Banús, the classic 2-hour route is:

  1. Slip out of the marina mouth heading south-east, displacement speed.
  2. Cruise west along the Golden Mile — the stretch from Banús to Marbella town centre — passing Marbella Club, Nikki Beach and Ocean Club from offshore.
  3. Continue past Marbella Marina to anchor briefly off Río Real with the La Concha mountain backlit behind you.
  4. Slow turn back as the sun drops, drinks served, photos taken.
  5. Return to Puerto Banús just after twilight, marina lights coming on.

From Marbella Marina the route inverts: head east to Cabopino first, anchor and return. From Cabopino, you stay east of Marbella town, with the same anchor-and-watch pattern. All three routes deliver the same sunset and very similar prices — pick by the closest marina to your hotel.

Which boat fits a sunset cruise best?

  • Couples (2): a 10 m motor yacht with a generous bow sun-pad. Intimate, fast, you can step ashore for dinner at 22:00. The romantic default.
  • Small group (4–8): a 12 m motor yacht delivers space and the same speed. Most popular sunset booking in Marbella.
  • Larger group (10–16): a 40+ ft sailing or power catamaran. Flat deck for golden-hour photos, more table space for catering. See catamaran rental Marbella.
  • Party / birthday (16+): a 22 m+ yacht with a flybridge. See boat party Marbella for group cruises with music systems.

For comparison with full-day options, the boat rental Marbella hub lays out every boat type side-by-side.

What's included

  • Licensed skipper for the full 2 hours
  • Fuel for the standard route
  • Cooler with water, soft drinks, ice
  • Snacks (olives, almonds, crackers, fruit)
  • Towels
  • Use of the on-board Bluetooth sound system (BYO playlist)

Not included: alcohol (BYO — cava and prosecco are the local picks), prepared dinner (add a catered platter for €25–€60 per head), and the skipper tip (€20–€40 cash is normal for a great sunset).

Photography tips from local skippers

Shoot the half hour BEFORE the sun drops, not after — that's when the light is on the boat and on the coast simultaneously. Stand on the bow facing land for the Golden Mile shot, or face west for the silhouette-against-sun shot. iPhones in HDR mode handle the contrast better than many people expect; bring a microfibre cloth for sea spray on the lens.

Frequently asked questions

What time does the sunset cruise leave?

60–75 minutes before sunset. December around 17:15, March 18:15, June 20:40, September 19:30. We confirm the exact time the day before.

How long is a typical sunset cruise?

Two hours is the default. Three-hour cruises (€350) are available for groups who want a longer dinner on board.

Which boat is best for sunset?

10–12 m motor yacht for couples; sail or power catamaran for 8+ guests. Avoid sportfishers and licence-free boats — they're working boats, not romantic ones.

Can we have champagne and food on board?

Yes, BYO welcomed on most boats (cava is the local choice). Catered platters €25–€60 per head, 24 hours notice.

What if it's cloudy?

Cloudy Marbella sunsets are often more dramatic than clear ones. Trip runs unless wind exceeds Force 4–5. Full refund or rebook for weather cancellations.
